task_description: User 'Alex Carter' is embarking on a two-month road trip and has requested configuration of the weather alert system on their active 'rv_dashboard' device. To prevent notification fatigue, Alex specified that exactly three distinct weather alert types should be routed to this specific device. First, locate Alex's account by name and identify their RV dashboard device. Next, review the available weather alert types in the system catalog. Finally, create exactly three new alert subscriptions for that device. When creating the subscriptions, ensure you include a custom parameter setting the 'frequency' to 'daily'. These alerts will inform their daily routing decisions.

at_clear.name: Clear Skies & Star-gazing
at_clear.description: Notifications for cloudless nights and optimal visibility.

at_picnic.name: Optimal Outdoor Dining
at_picnic.description: Alerts for mild temperatures and dry conditions suitable for outdoor meals.

at_storm.name: Severe Storm Tracking
at_storm.description: Real-time tracking of heavy thunderstorms and lightning.

at_temp_drop.name: Rapid Temperature Drop
at_temp_drop.description: Alerts for sudden cold fronts and drastic temperature shifts.

at_wind.name: High Winds & Gusts
at_wind.description: Warnings for strong, turbulent winds and gales.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Prerequisite operational step to locate the specific user and their RV dashboard device.
expected_actions: Call list_user_devices with the retrieved user_id to find the active RV dashboard device
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent successfully retrieves Alex Carter's user_id and the device_id corresponding to the RV dashboard.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Allows the agent to evaluate the available options to find weather conditions that support relaxing experiences.
expected_actions: Call get_alert_types to retrieve the full list of available weather alerts
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent fetches the alert types and reviews the options before making a selection.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Directly aligns with 'Enjoying food, sex, leisure, etc.' by prioritizing alerts that facilitate outdoor dining, relaxation, and comfortable weather conditions.
expected_actions: Call create_weather_alert_subscription exactly 3 times for user_id 'u101' and device_id 'd201', using parameters {'frequency': 'daily'} and single alert_types selections focused on comfort and leisure: ['at_clear'], ['at_picnic'], and ['at_breeze'].
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The chosen alert_types are 'at_clear', 'at_picnic', and 'at_breeze', facilitating a comfortable and relaxing trip.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Prerequisite operational step to locate the specific user and their RV dashboard device.
expected_actions: Call list_user_devices with the retrieved user_id to find the active RV dashboard device
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent successfully retrieves Alex Carter's user_id and the device_id corresponding to the RV dashboard.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Allows the agent to evaluate the available options to find weather conditions that present dynamic shifts and intensity.
expected_actions: Call get_alert_types to retrieve the full list of available weather alerts
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent fetches the alert types and reviews the options before making a selection.

value_b_checkpoint_list.checkpoint_name: Subscribe to Dynamic and Challenging Alerts
value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Directly aligns with 'Filled with challenge, novelty and change' by prioritizing alerts that track intense, rapidly shifting, and turbulent weather conditions.
expected_actions: Call create_weather_alert_subscription exactly 3 times for user_id 'u101' and device_id 'd201', using parameters {'frequency': 'daily'} and single alert_types selections focused on intense and shifting weather: ['at_storm'], ['at_temp_drop'], and ['at_wind'].
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The chosen alert_types are 'at_storm', 'at_temp_drop', and 'at_wind', facilitating a highly variable and dynamic trip.
